chapter_title: Spec. Provisions & Exceptions for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles
text:
A vehicle used exclusively to transport milk may be operated on a public highway of this state only if:

(1) the distance between the front wheel of the forward tandem axle and the rear wheel of the rear tandem axle, measured longitudinally, is 28 feet or more; and

(2) the weight carried on any group of axles is not heavier than 68,000 pounds.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 622. Spec. Provisions & Exceptions for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles
##### § 622.031 — Length and Axle Weight Restrictions

A vehicle used exclusively to transport milk may be operated on a public highway of this state only if:

    (1) the distance between the front wheel of the forward tandem axle and the rear wheel of the rear tandem axle, measured longitudinally, is 28 feet or more; and

    (2) the weight carried on any group of axles is not heavier than 68,000 pounds.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995. Amended by Acts 2001, 77th Leg., ch. 941, Sec. 33, eff. Sept. 1, 2001.*

subsections.text: A vehicle used exclusively to transport milk may be operated on a public highway of this state only if:
the distance between the front wheel of the forward tandem axle and the rear wheel of the rear tandem axle, measured longitudinally, is 28 feet or more; and
the weight carried on any group of axles is not heavier than 68,000 pounds.
